content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

January 18, 1995

Michael J. Noland
Kahn, Soares & Conway
219 North Douty Street
Post Office Box 1376
Hanford, California 93232

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-94-394

Dear Mr. Noland:

You have asked for advice on behalf of Hanford City Councilmember Tamie Pick regarding the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1

QUESTION

May Councilmember Pick participate in council meetings and
discussions regarding labor negotiations and contracts with city
employees represented by the California League of City Employees
Associations, Inc. ("CLOCEA")?

CONCLUSION

Ms. Pick may participate in council deliberations and decisions regarding the labor contracts. The Act's conflict-of-interest provisions do not require Ms. Pick to disqualify herself from
participating in these decisions because under the Act, she has no
financial interest in the matter.

FACTS

Tamie Pick was elected to the Hanford City Council on
November 8, 1994, and sworn into office on December 6, 1994.
Ms. Pick is currently an employee of Kings County, California. As
a county employee, Tamie Pick is a dues paying member of CLOCEA.
CLOCEA represents various Kings County employee groups in labor

1
Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations, Sections
18000-18954. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

File No. A-94-394
Page 2


negotiations with the county. Councilmember Pick is not an of-
ficer of CLOCEA and she does not directly participate in labor
negotiations between CLOCEA and Kings County.

CLOCEA also represents the City of Hanford firefighters and
the city of Hanford general employees in their contract
negotiations with the city. The labor contract between the
firefighters and the city expired on December 31, 1994, and is
currently up for negotiation. The general employees' contract
with the city expires on December 31, 1995. The Hanford city
manager oversees contract negotiations on behalf of the city and
will report to the council on the status of the negotiations. The
council will review the contract proposals and vote to approve or
disapprove the final contract.

ANALYSIS

Section 87100 prohibits public officials from making,
participating in making, or otherwise using their official posi-
tion to influence a governmental decision in which they have a
financial interest. Councilmember Pick is a public official who
will be participating in a governmental decision. (Section 82048
and Regulation 18700).

An official has a financial interest in a decision within the
meaning of Section 87100 if it is reasonably foreseeable that the
decision will have a material financial effect, distinguishable
from its effect on the public generally, on the official, on a
member of his or her immediate family, or on:

(a) Any business entity in which the public
official has a direct or indirect investment worth one
thousand dollars ($1,000) or more.

(b) Any real property in which the public
official has a direct or indirect interest worth one
thousand dollars ($1,000) or more.

(c) Any source of income, other than gifts and
other than loans by a commercial lending institution in
the regular course of business on terms available to the
public without regard to official status, aggregating
two hundred fifty dollars ($250) or more in value
provided to, received by or promised to the public
official within 12 months prior to the time when the
decision is made.

(d) Any business entity in which the public
official is a director, officer, partner, trustee,
employee, or holds any position of management. Section
87103 (a)-(d).

File No. A-94-394
Page 3


Councilmember Pick, however, does not have a financial interest in the council's negotiations and decisions regarding the firefighter or general employees' labor contracts merely because she is employed by Kings County and is a member of CLOCEA. Under the Act's definitions, income does not include any salary or reimbursement for expenses received from a state, local, or federal government agency. (Section 82030(b)(2).) Ms. Pick's salary as a county employee does not constitute income under the Act, and thus would not create a financial interest needed for the conflict of interest provisions to apply.

The fact that Ms. Pick is a member of CLOCEA does not give rise to any conflicts either. Section 87103(d) of the Act set forth above provides that a financial interest includes a business entity in which the public official is a director, officer, partner, trustee, employee, or holds any position of management. However, CLOCEA is not a business entity, since it is a not-for-profit organization, and Ms. Pick does not hold any management position in the organization, being only a dues paying member. (Section 82005.)

If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at 916/322-5901.


Sincerely,

Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel

[signature]

By: Hyla P. Wagner
    Counsel, Legal Division
